title: Server-Side Rendering (SSR) Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of SSR benefits like SEO/FCP, Hydration, and Next.js.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary benefit of SSR for search engine optimization (SEO)?
options: It makes the app faster to build.
It provides pre-rendered HTML content that can be easily crawled and indexed by search engines.
It hides all JavaScript code from the user.
None of the above.

questions.question: What is the name of the process where React takes over the static HTML sent by the server and makes it interactive?
options: Hydration
Serialization
Compilation
Reactivation

questions.question: Which of the following describes the difference between SSR and CSR (Client-Side Rendering)?
options: SSR renders the page in the browser, while CSR renders it on the server.
SSR renders the page on the server, while CSR renders it in the browser using JavaScript.
There is no difference in rendering location.
Only CSR can be used with React.

questions.question: Which modern React framework is most commonly used for handling SSR and dynamic data fetching?
options: Vue.js
Angular
Next.js
Express.js

questions.question: Does SSR improve the First Contentful Paint (FCP) for users on slow networks?
options: Yes, as they see the initial HTML content sooner.
No, it actually slows down the first paint.
Only if you use class-based syntax.
Only if you return them with an ID.
